title: BudsAuth: Toward Gesture-Wise Continuous User Authentication Through Earbuds Vibration Sensing

method.input_representation: 6-channel IMU time-series (accelerometer x,y,z and gyroscope x,y,z)
method.training_strategy: Pre-training a general feature encoder via DAL to suppress gesture variance, followed by personalized fine-tuning via transfer learning for registered users.

evaluation.context: Achieved using continuous authentication with weighted voting over 7 consecutive gesture samples.

evaluation_protocol: Data collected in two sessions; Session 1 used for training and Session 2 for testing. Used a leave-one-out approach where 15 users pre-trained the encoder and 5 were tested as legitimate users vs spoofers.

usability.usability_issues: Requires active on-face gestures to trigger authentication.

key_contribution: Introduced the first user authentication system based on on-face interaction vibrations captured by earbud IMUs
Proposed a CNN-based encoder with Domain Adversarial Learning to separate user identity from gesture type
Implemented a distance-based weighted voting scheme for high-precision continuous authentication
main_strength: Extremely low EER for continuous authentication
Low computational and energy overhead for mobile deployment
main_weakness: Data collected in a highly controlled seated environment
Lack of empirical testing against realistic motion noise or music interference
insights.notes_for_review: The system relies on users performing specific gestures to be authenticated; it is not completely 'passive' in the sense that it requires these interaction events to occur.
